featured.name: LocalCowork
featured.tagline: Privacy-first agent workflows powered by LFM2-24B-A2B running entirely on-device.
featured.why_radar: Finally, someone gets it. Enterprise workflows that never touch the cloud, never leak data, and never depend on API uptime. Liquid AI's approach of optimising for local tool dispatch could be the architecture that makes AI agents genuinely enterprise-ready.

featured.name: Symphony
featured.tagline: Open-source framework for orchestrating autonomous AI coding agents through structured implementation runs.
featured.why_radar: OpenAI goes full circle. They helped create the autonomous coding agent space, now they're open-sourcing the infrastructure to manage it properly. Building this on Elixir and BEAM shows they're thinking about reliability and concurrency first, which is exactly what you need when agents are touching your production code.

picks.name: gws
picks.tagline: CLI tool providing unified interface to Google Workspace APIs for humans and AI agents.
picks.why_radar: Google finally admits that REST endpoints and OAuth flows are a pain. This CLI approach makes Workspace APIs actually usable for quick automation and agent integration. Smart move making it agent-friendly from day one.

picks.name: Cursor Automations
picks.tagline: Agentic coding tool that automatically launches agents triggered by code changes, Slack messages, or timers.
picks.why_radar: Cursor keeps pushing the boundary of what coding tools can do. Automations that respond to external triggers feel like the natural evolution of AI coding assistants. This could be the missing piece that makes AI agents truly integrated into development workflows.

picks.name: Luma Agents
picks.tagline: Creative AI agents powered by Unified Intelligence models for end-to-end multimedia content generation.
picks.why_radar: Luma's betting that creative workflows need coordination between text, images, video, and audio rather than separate tools for each. Their 'Unified Intelligence' approach could solve the fragmentation problem that makes AI creative work feel so disjointed.

picks.name: GPT-5.4
picks.tagline: OpenAI's most capable frontier model combining coding, reasoning, and computer use in Pro and Thinking versions.
picks.why_radar: OpenAI's consolidation play. Instead of separate models for different tasks, GPT-5.4 promises to do everything in one package. The real test will be whether unified capability actually beats specialised models in practice.

hn_top5.title: Show HN: Moongate – Ultima Online server emulator in .NET 10 with Lua scripting
hn_top5.summary: Someone built a modern Ultima Online server emulator using .NET 10 with Lua scripting support. This lets nostalgic gamers run their own private servers of the classic MMORPG with modern tooling. It's impressive technical work that will delight the dedicated UO community.

hn_top5.title: Ask HN: How are you using multi-agent AI systems in your daily workflow?
hn_top5.summary: The HN crowd is discussing how they integrate multi-agent AI systems into their work routines. Most responses are likely theoretical rather than practical implementations. The low engagement suggests few people are actually using these systems meaningfully yet.

hn_top5.title: Show HN: Deterministic browser control for AI agents (~90% on Mind2Web)
hn_top5.summary: A developer created a browser control system specifically for AI agents that achieves 90% accuracy on the Mind2Web benchmark. This addresses the real problem of AI agents struggling to reliably interact with web interfaces. The low comment count suggests the technical achievement might be flying under the radar.

hn_top5.title: Show HN: NERDs – Entity-centered long-term memory for LLM agents
hn_top5.summary: NERDs provides persistent memory for AI agents by organizing information around entities rather than simple chat history. This tackles the fundamental limitation of LLMs forgetting context over long conversations. The concept is promising but needs more real-world validation.

hn_top5.title: Show HN: Steadwing – Your Autonomous On-Call Engineer
hn_top5.summary: Steadwing claims to be an autonomous on-call engineer that handles incidents without human intervention. The promise sounds too good to be true for complex production systems. Most engineering teams won't trust critical incidents to an AI system anytime soon.
